_comment: Device profiles for Air Twin. All device-specific facts live here. The twin engine reads from this at runtime â€” no device knowledge is baked into engine logic. Room-specific values (volume, efficiency factor) live in config.json and asset_registry.json.

ikea_starkvind_e2007._comment: IKEA STARKVIND Air Purifier E2007. SKU 004.619.49 (standalone unit, particle filter included, gas filter optional/sold separately).

auto._comment: Auto mode uses a finer 1-9 internal scale distinct from manual 1-5. Steps are not mapped to manual equivalents — twin builds separate empirical performance curves per auto step.

cadr._comment: Clean Air Delivery Rate in m3/h per fan speed, per filter configuration. Speeds 1 and 5 are IKEA/AHAM published values. Speeds 2-4 are not published by IKEA â€” see interpolation_policy. Source: IKEA product page for SKU 004.619.49 and regional IKEA sites (SE) which publish in m3/h natively.
cadr._interpolation_comment: Power-law interpolation anchored to published endpoints. Fan motor airflow scales approximately with RPM; power scales with RPM^3. Linear interpolation is not physically correct for this device class. Twin engine applies lower confidence to commanded speeds 2-4 until empirical observations are available to replace interpolated priors. Empirical values for speeds 2-4 accumulate in twin_state.json and supersede these interpolated values once evidence threshold is met. Published anchor values for speeds 1 and 5 are never superseded by empirical observations â€” consistent field deviation from anchors is interpreted as a room efficiency factor, not a device CADR correction.

particle_filter_only._comment: Particle filter installed, no gas filter. This is the factory default configuration for SKU 004.619.49.

2.source_detail: Not published by IKEA. Twin engine interpolates using power_law policy between speeds 1 and 5.

3.source_detail: Not published by IKEA. Twin engine interpolates using power_law policy between speeds 1 and 5.

4.source_detail: Not published by IKEA. Twin engine interpolates using power_law policy between speeds 1 and 5.

particle_and_gas_filter._comment: Both particle and gas filter installed. Gas filter adds restriction, reducing CADR. Filter for gas cleaning sold separately (IKEA STARKVIND Filter for gas cleaning).

2.source_detail: Not published by IKEA. Twin engine interpolates using power_law policy between speeds 1 and 5.

3.source_detail: Not published by IKEA. Twin engine interpolates using power_law policy between speeds 1 and 5.

4.source_detail: Not published by IKEA. Twin engine interpolates using power_law policy between speeds 1 and 5.

noise_db._comment: Noise level in dB per filter configuration. Published by IKEA for speeds 1 and 5 only. Informational â€” not used in twin engine calculations.

power_consumption_w._comment: Power consumption in Watts per filter configuration. Published by IKEA for speeds 1 and 5 only. Power scales approximately with RPM^3 â€” intermediate values can be estimated from smart plug measurement at each fan speed and used to empirically anchor intermediate CADR estimates before sufficient particle decay observations exist.

particle_only.label: Particle filter only
particle_only.description: Factory default. Filters PM2.5, dust, pollen. Rated 99.5% efficiency for particles 0.1-2.5 micrometers.
particle_only._filter_life_comment: IKEA recommends replacement within 6 months. 6 months * 730 hours/month = 4380 hours. This is a manufacturer time-based heuristic, not a performance-based threshold. Twin engine performance ratio is a more reliable degradation signal and takes precedence. filter_life_hours is used as a soft reference only.

particle_and_gas.label: Particle and gas filter
particle_and_gas.description: Particle filter plus optional gas filter installed. Additionally filters formaldehyde, VOCs, cooking odours, smoke odours.
particle_and_gas._filter_life_comment: Same manufacturer 6-month recommendation applies to both filter types. Gas filter replacement interval may differ in high-VOC environments â€” not separately specified by IKEA.

ikea_starkvind_e2007._default_filter_comment: SKU 004.619.49 ships with particle filter only. Twin uses this as the default until a filter change is logged via QR maintenance event.

performance_model._comment: Parameters governing how the twin engine models and evaluates purifier performance.
performance_model._formula_comment: Expected decay rate in m3/h/m3 = volumes per hour. room_efficiency_factor starts at 1.0 and is updated by the twin engine from performance_observations. It lives in twin_state.json, not here. Consistent field deviation from manufacturer CADR anchors is absorbed into room_efficiency_factor, not into the device profile.
performance_model._perf_ratio_comment: Performance ratio below this value triggers performance degradation diagnostic. Ratio = observed_decay / expected_decay. 1.0 = performing to spec. Values below 0.70 warrant investigation.
performance_model._empirical_comment: Minimum number of performance_observations events required before empirical CADR estimates for speeds 2-4 are promoted over interpolated priors in twin_state.json.
performance_model._physics_comment: Starkvind uses fixed fan speed steps. Motor runs at fixed RPM and does NOT compensate for filter restriction. Clogged filter degrades performance ratio proportionally across all fan speeds. Severe late-stage clog may cause high speeds to fail first (motor stall / thermal protection) while low speeds still function. Gradual onset correlating with twin_filter_age is the expected signature.
performance_model._auto_mode_comment: No manufacturer CADR exists for auto mode steps 1-9. Performance assessment in auto mode withheld until sufficient empirical observations exist per step. Manual mode uses manufacturer CADR anchors from day one.

control._ack_comment: Twin engine sends command, waits this many seconds, verifies reported state changed. Retries once. Flags unresponsive if no confirmation after retry.

physical._coverage_comment: IKEA states up to 215 sq ft (approx 20 m2) depending on fan speed. This is a marketing figure based on the well-mixed room model. Twin engine uses CADR / room_volume for actual performance modelling.
